name: DIGITAL OVERLAY
pitch: It takes a few hits to break. Each click shakes the pixels around it and leaves them raised; land the hits in quick succession and on the third the image gives way — shattering from the break point, tumbling under gravity, then spiralling back inward as one vortex and dissolving seamlessly back together. Pause between hits and the pixels settle and it stays whole.

lifecycle.idle: A slow soft sheen of light sweeps across the surface with a faint breathing — alive but calm
lifecycle.discovery: On hover the whole image leans toward the cursor in a gentle 3D parallax and the sheen brightens. Each hit shakes the surrounding pixels and leaves them raised as 3D tiles for ~2s; they settle back if you pause, so the image only breaks under quick successive hits
lifecycle.buildUp: The final hit gives way — the image shatters from the break point, tiles blasting outward with motion blur and tumbling under gravity
lifecycle.peak: All tiles piled at the bottom, scattered, rotated and desaturated
lifecycle.recovery: Tiles spiral inward as one coherent vortex — centre-first, outer tiles trailing as arms — the bevel and glow dissolving away so the picture reforms seamlessly
